title: Oreogrammitis reducta
taxonomy: Oreogrammitis reducta is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It belongs to the genus Oreogrammitis, which is a group of ferns that are native to tropical regions. The species is classified as a terrestrial fern, meaning it grows on land rather than in water. The classification of Oreogrammitis reducta is further defined by its unique characteristics, such as its frond shape and leaf arrangement. This classification is used to identify and distinguish Oreogrammitis reducta from other similar species within the Polypodiaceae family.

The plant's leaves and stems are used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Oreogrammitis reducta is also believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce swelling and pain. intro: Oreogrammitis reducta, also known as the New Guinea fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Polypodiaceae, specifically a type of epiphytic fern. The New Guinea fern is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and reducing inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Oreogrammitis reducta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties.
